briefdescription.para: The bit in the SDO COB-ID specifying whether the SDO exists and is valid.
The bit in the SDO COB-ID specifying whether to use an 11-bit (0) or 29-bit (1) CAN-ID.
The data type (and object index) of an SDO parameter record.
The static initializer for struct co_sdo_par.
SDO abort code: Toggle bit not altered.
SDO abort code: SDO protocol timed out.
SDO abort code: Client/server command specifier not valid or unknown.
SDO abort code: Invalid block size (block mode only).
SDO abort code: Invalid sequence number (block mode only).
SDO abort code: CRC error (block mode only).
SDO abort code: Out of memory.
SDO abort code: Unsupported access to an object.
SDO abort code: Attempt to read a write only object.
SDO abort code: Attempt to write a read only object.
SDO abort code: Object does not exist in the object dictionary.
SDO abort code: Object cannot be mapped to the PDO.
SDO abort code: The number and length of the objects to be mapped would exceed the PDO length.
SDO abort code: General parameter incompatibility reason.
SDO abort code: General internal incompatibility in the device.
SDO abort code: Access failed due to a hardware error.
SDO abort code: Data type does not match, length of service parameter does not match.
SDO abort code: Data type does not match, length of service parameter too high.
SDO abort code: Data type does not match, length of service parameter too low.
SDO abort code: Sub-index does not exist.
SDO abort code: Invalid value for parameter (download only).
SDO abort code: Value of parameter written too high (download only).
SDO abort code: Value of parameter written too low (download only).
SDO abort code: Maximum value is less than minimum value (download only).
SDO abort code: Resource not available: SDO connection.
SDO abort code: General error.
SDO abort code: Data cannot be transferred or stored to the application.
SDO abort code: Data cannot be transferred or stored to the application because of local control.
SDO abort code: Data cannot be transferred or stored to the application because of the present device state.
SDO abort code: Object dictionary dynamic generation fails or no object dictionary is present (e.g.

detaileddescription.para: object dictionary is generated from file and generation fails because of a file error).

briefdescription.para: SDO abort code: No data available.
The default size (in bytes) of an SDO upload/download request memory buffer in the absence of dynamic memory allocation.

detaileddescription.para: The default size is large enough to accomodate all basic data types.

briefdescription.para: The maximum number of Client/Server-SDOs.
The static initializer for struct co_sdo_req.
Returns a string describing an SDO abort code.
Initializes a CANopen SDO upload/download request.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen SDO download request.
a pointer to the memory buffer used to store the serialized value. If NULL, an internal buffer is used.

briefdescription.para: Finalizes a CANopen SDO upload/download request.
Clears a CANopen SDO upload/download request, including its buffer.
Returns true if the specified request includes the first segment, and false otherwise.
Returns true if the specified request includes the last segment, and false otherwise.
Copies the next segment of the specified CANopen SDO download request to the internal buffer and, on the last segment, returns the buffer.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen SDO download request.
the address of a pointer which, on success, points to the first byte in the buffer (can be NULL).
the address of a value which, on success, contains the total number of bytes in the buffer (can be NULL).
the address of a value which, on error, contains the SDO abort code (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 if all segments have been copied, and -1 if one or more segments remain or an error has occurred. In the latter case, *pac contains the SDO abort code.

briefdescription.para: Copies the next segment of the specified CANopen SDO download request to the internal buffer and, on the last segment, reads the value.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen SDO download request.
the data type of the value.
the address of the value to be downloaded (can be NULL).
the address of a value which, on error, contains the SDO abort code (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 if all segments have been copied and the value has been read, and -1 if one or more segments remain or an error has occurred. In the latter case, *pac contains the SDO abort code.

briefdescription.para: Copies the next segment of the specified CANopen SDO download request to the internal buffer and, on the last segment, writes the value to the specified file.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen SDO download request.
a pointer to the name of the file.
the address of a value which, on error, contains the SDO abort code (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 if all segments have been copied and the value has been read, and -1 if one or more segments remain or an error has occurred. In the latter case, *pac contains the SDO abort code.

briefdescription.para: Writes the specified bytes to a buffer and constructs a CANopen SDO upload request.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen SDO upload request.
a pointer to the bytes to be uploaded. It is the responsibility of the user to ensure that the buffer remains valid until the request completes.
the number of bytes at ptr.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct co_sdo_req *req, co_unsigned16_t type, const void *val, co_unsigned32_t *pac)
memberdef.name: co_sdo_req_up_val

briefdescription.para: Writes the specified value to a buffer and constructs a CANopen SDO upload request.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen SDO upload request.
the data type of the value.
the address of the value to be uploaded. In case of string or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.
the address of a value which, on error, contains the SDO abort code (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, *pac contains the SDO abort code.

briefdescription.para: Loads the specified file into a buffer and constructs a CANopen SDO upload request.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen SDO upload request.
a pointer to the name of the file.
the address of a value which, on error, contains the SDO abort code (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, *pac contains the SDO abort code.

briefdescription.para: This header file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the Service Data Object (SDO) declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
